title: Namespace installed Make targets and harden onboarding docs

riskAssessment.reason: Renaming installed Make targets affects every registered project check and onboarding command.

executionDecision.reason: The review provides a reproducible Make recipe override and a concrete namespace remedy.

preReviewWarnings: Verify upgrades and fresh installs, and ensure no legacy generic recipe remains in Makefile.ai.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: Installed Makefile.ai defines only ai-cockpit-* recipes and cannot override common host project targets.
Installation fails before writing when the host already defines a reserved ai-cockpit-* target.
Check Registry, readiness, CI examples, and docs use ai-cockpit-quality consistently.
Archive immutability and recommended-command behavior are described accurately.
Japanese confidence terminology is 信頼度 and core lifecycle failure paths gain stronger coverage.
Fresh install, upgrade, quality, lifecycle, and aggregate PR checks pass.
guidelines: Never rely on GNU Make recipe override order.
Keep project-owned quality commands configurable through Makefile.ai.stack variables.
Fail before writing when a reserved target collision is detected.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: The user explicitly requested correction of the reviewed installation and Make compatibility risks.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
